title: coleus divaricatus
intro: Coleus divaricatus, also known as Painted Nettle or Country Borage, is a flowering plant species native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Africa. The leaves and roots of Coleus divaricatus have been used in traditional medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The plant has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
